prompt: Generate documentation for the 'order-cancellation' feature. The state file at .feature-docs/_state/order-cancellation.md contains a fully traced and resolved feature with entry point POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel, service layer trace through OrderCancellationService, outbound call to payment-service resolved, and OrderCancelledEvent published. Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Output contains zero fenced code blocks (no ``` markers anywhere)
Output contains zero inline code (no backtick-wrapped text)
Output does not contain file paths like src/main/java or .java or .ts extensions
Output contains no language identifiers after code fences (no ```java, ```json, etc.)

prompt: Generate documentation for a 'user-registration' feature. State file contains: POST /api/users/register entry point, UserRegistrationService.register orchestrates flow, validates email uniqueness, hashes password via PasswordEncoder, persists via UserRepository, publishes UserRegisteredEvent, outbound call to email-service for verification email (resolved). Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Output does not contain the phrase 'delve into'
Output does not contain 'it's important to note' or 'it is important to note'
Output does not contain the word 'leverage'
Output does not contain the word 'utilize'
Output does not contain 'in order to'
Output does not contain the word 'comprehensive'
Output does not contain the word 'robust'
Output does not contain the word 'seamless'
Output does not contain the word 'ensure'
Output does not contain the word 'facilitate'

prompt: Generate documentation for 'payment-processing' feature. All dependencies fully resolved. State file contains: POST /api/payments/charge entry point, PaymentService.processCharge orchestrates, calls StripeGateway.charge (outbound, resolved -- StripeGateway validates card, creates charge, returns confirmation), persists via PaymentRepository, publishes PaymentCompletedEvent. Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Resolved dependency sections do not use the word 'might'
Resolved dependency sections do not use the word 'possibly'
Output does not contain 'could potentially' in resolved sections
Output does not contain 'appears to' in resolved sections
Output does not contain 'seems to' in resolved sections
Resolved cross-service dependency descriptions use definitive language (does, sends, returns, validates)

prompt: Generate documentation for 'inventory-management' feature. State file contains: GET /api/inventory/{sku} handled by InventoryController.getStock, PUT /api/inventory/{sku}/adjust handled by InventoryController.adjustStock. Service layer: InventoryService.checkAvailability, InventoryService.adjustStock, WarehouseAllocationService.reallocate. Entities: InventoryItem, WarehouseAllocation. Event: StockAdjustedEvent. Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Output references InventoryController by name
Output references InventoryService by name
Output references WarehouseAllocationService by name
Output references StockAdjustedEvent by name
Output references /api/inventory/{sku} and /api/inventory/{sku}/adjust by their actual paths
Output does not use generic references like 'the inventory service' or 'the controller' without the actual class name on first mention

prompt: Generate documentation for 'order-cancellation' feature. State file contains: POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel, OrderCancellationService.cancel validates order exists, validates order is cancellable (status must be pending or confirmed), validates cancellation window not expired, calls payment-service for refund (resolved), saves cancelled status, publishes OrderCancelledEvent. Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Output describes what happens when the order does not exist
Output describes what happens when the order status is not cancellable (e.g., already shipped)
Output describes what happens when the cancellation window has expired
Output describes what happens when the payment service refund call fails
Output contains an Error Handling or Edge Cases section
Error flows are not relegated to a brief footnote but given substantive coverage

prompt: Generate documentation for 'user-registration' feature. State file contains full trace: POST /api/users/register, UserRegistrationService handles email validation, password hashing, persistence, welcome email via email-service (resolved). Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: The Overview or first section explains the business purpose of user registration before describing mechanics
A Business Context section exists and precedes the Feature Flow section
Each major section opens with why/context before describing how/what

prompt: Generate documentation for 'order-cancellation' feature with full trace and resolved deps. Settings: format=notion, tone=conversational.

assertions.text: Output uses <details><summary> toggle blocks for entry points or dependencies
Output uses blockquote callouts (> [!NOTE] or > [!WARNING] or > [!TIP])

assertions.text: Output contains a {toc} macro near the top

prompt: Generate documentation for 'payment-processing' feature with full trace. Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Output uses third person ('the system', 'the service') not second person ('you')
Output uses complete sentences, not fragments or bullet-only sections in prose areas

Output avoids contractions (uses 'does not' instead of 'doesn't', etc.) or uses them sparingly

prompt: Generate documentation for 'payment-processing' feature with full trace. Settings: format=markdown, tone=terse.

assertions.text: No emojis appear in any headings

prompt: Generate documentation for 'order-fulfillment' feature. State: phase=resolving, 2 resolved deps (inventory-service, shipping-service), 1 unresolved dep (analytics-service POST /api/events -- not in registry). Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Resolved dependencies (inventory-service, shipping-service) are described with definitive language
The unresolved dependency (analytics-service) section uses hedging language ('may', 'likely', 'appears to') since behavior is unconfirmed
The unresolved dependency is clearly marked as unresolved or unverified
Output includes a note that some dependencies could not be fully traced

prompt: Generate documentation for 'checkout' feature. Trace shows: CheckoutService in order-service calls payment-service POST /api/charges (resolved) and inventory-service PUT /api/inventory/{sku}/reserve (resolved). Payment-service in turn calls fraud-service POST /api/fraud/check (resolved at depth 2). Settings: format=markdown, tone=professional.

assertions.text: Every cross-service call explicitly names both the calling service and the target service
Dependencies are described in execution order (payment before inventory if that's the trace order), not alphabetical
The transitive dependency (fraud-service called by payment-service) is documented, not just direct deps
The documentation makes clear that fraud-service is a second-level dependency (called by payment-service, not directly by checkout)
